---
title: "Official Statistics"
author: "Dr Jerin Paul  \nAssistant Professor  \nDepartment of Statistics  \nVimala
  College (Autonomous), Thrissur  \n"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %Y')`"
output:
  word_document: default
  pdf_document: default
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
documentclass: book
bibliography: references.bib
biblio-style: apalike
link-citations: true
description: Official Statistics textbook for Calicut University FYUGP
subtitle: "A Textbook for FYUGP Programme  \nVth Sem BSc Statistics,  \nVimala College
  (Autonomous), Thrissur  \n"
geometry: "top=2.5cm, bottom=2.5cm, left=2.5cm, right=2.5cm"
fontsize: 12pt
linestretch: 1.5
---

# Preface {-}

This textbook has been written to meet the requirements of the **Four Year Undergraduate Programme (FYUGP)** of Calicut University for the course on **Official Statistics**.

The book is organised into four chapters aligned with the Calicut University FYUGP syllabus:

| Chapter | Title |
|---------|-------|
| 1 | Introduction |
| 2 | Population and Economic Statistics |
| 3 | Economic Development and National Income |
| 4 | Measuring Inequality and Poverty |

Each chapter opens with learning objectives. Key terms are **bolded** when first introduced.

## Acknowledgements {-}

The author acknowledges data sources used in this book: the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI), the Registrar General of India, the Reserve Bank of India, and the United Nations Statistics Division.

## Course Information {-}

### Course Details {-}

| | |
|---|---|
| **Programme** | B.Sc. Statistics |
| **Course Code** | STA5EJ305 |
| **Course Title** | Official Statistics |
| **Type of Course** | Major Elective |
| **Semester** | V |
| **Academic Level** | 300–399 |

### Credit Structure {-}

| Credit | Lecture/Week | Tutorial/Week | Practical/Week | Total Hours |
|:------:|:------------:|:-------------:|:--------------:|:-----------:|
| 4 | 4 | — | — | 60 |

**Pre-requisites:** Basic Statistics

**Course Objective:** Aware students of the role of Statistics in National Policy Formulation, Planning, and framing of various policies by the Governments.

### Detailed Syllabus {-}

**Module I — Introduction (12 Hrs, 20 Marks)**

1. Introduction to Indian and International Statistical systems.
2. Methods of collection of official statistics.
3. Role, function and activities of Central and State Statistical organizations.
4. Organization of large-scale sample surveys. Role of Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation (MoSPI).
5. Central Statistical Office (CSO), National Sample Survey Office (NSSO), and National Statistical Commission.
6. Government of India's principal publications containing data on topics such as population, industry and finance.
7. Scope and contents of population census of India.

**Module II — Population and Economic Statistics (12 Hrs, 20 Marks)**

8. Population growth in developed and developing countries.
9. Evaluation of performance of family welfare programmes.
10. Projections of labour force and manpower.
11. Statistics related to industries, foreign trade, balance of payment.
12. Statistics related to cost of living, inflation, educational and other social statistics.

**Module III — Economic Development and National Income (12 Hrs, 15 Marks)**

13. Economic development.
14. Growth in per capita income and distributive justice; indices of development.
15. Human Development Index.
16. National income estimation — Product approach.
17. National income estimation — Income approach.
18. National income estimation — Expenditure approach.

**Module IV — Measuring Inequality and Poverty (12 Hrs, 15 Marks)**

19. Measuring inequality in incomes: Lorenz curve.
20. Gini Coefficient.
21. Theil's measure.
22. Poverty measurements: Different issues.
23. Measures of incidence and intensity of poverty.


### Books and References {-}

1. Guide to Official Statistics, CSO (1999).
2. Statistical System in India, CSO (1995).
3. Principles and Accommodation of National Population Census, UNESCO.
4. Monthly Statistics of Foreign Trade in India, DGCIS, Calcutta and other Government Publications.
5. Keyfitz, N. (1977). *Applied Mathematical Demography*. Springer Verlag.
6. Sen, A. (1977). *Poverty and Inequality*.
7. Chubey, P.K. (1995). *Poverty Measurement*. New Age International.
